Added row in table in AboutUs.md with my details.

Previously the document did not have my details.

Add details of the group member Lee Kuan Yi, with his name, GitHub link and individual md file.

Now, AboutUs.md has my name.
Update AboutUs.md

Add the Budget Reminder System to warn users they are exceeding budget if their remaining budget is $20 or less.

Users will be notified if they are on the verge of exceeding their budget, so that they will be more cautious of their spending.

Additionally, updated showAllExpenses to display the total expenditure for all expenditures when command "view all" is entered and total expenditure for a category of expenditures when command "view [category]" is entered.
Fix a minor coding standard violation in the if statement in initLimit method in Limit.java

A minor fix as part of an attempt to fix GitHub's checks failure.

EXPECTED.TXT was out of date prior to this change.

Change EXPECTED.TXT to reflect the correct expected output of Finbro.

Also changed Budget Reminder message back as it was not the problem.
Budget Reminder System

commitResults.messageTitle: Convert Expense to record and update usages
commitResults.messageBody:
Refactor Expense from a POJO/class to a Java record (Expense(double,String,String)) and adjust all call sites to use record accessors (amount(), category(), date()).

Updated Storage, Ui, and ExpenseList to use the new accessors and updated unit tests accordingly.
Refactor Expense to record and update related components
Update ExpenseTest to use new methods of record Expense.
Update ExpenseTest.java

commitResults.messageTitle: Change string in ExpenseTest to block string
Add Javadoc & update logic in BudgetWarningService

commitResults.messageBody:
Previously, users could only view all of their expenses or see their expenses of a single certain category. And often, these are all not sorted by date, amount and category.

Add FilterService to handle filtering by month, amount from largest to smallest, and category in alphabetical order. Modify ViewCommand.java to introduce optional -filter subcommand that works with both "view all" and "view [category]" commands.

Users can now view their expenses sorted by these orders to retrieve specific insights of their spending trends e.g. the day they spent the most.

Extract and resolve duplicate method in parseYearMonth in both FilterService and ExpenseList.

Also added author tags.
Add unit tests for ViewCommand class
Filter
Fix more coding violations still present in ViewCommand and FilterServiceTest
Enhance expense viewing with filtering options and unit tests
